project.title: Expose a lowStockThreshold field from the existing inventory service for store managers.

prd_intake.plain_language_problem: Expose a lowStockThreshold field from the existing inventory service for store managers.
desired_outcomes: Inventory service output includes lowStockThreshold for every item.
success_proof: A test can assert toInventoryItem returns lowStockThreshold from the source row.
boundaries: Do not change SKU or quantity semantics.

nontechnical_intake.plain_language_problem: Expose a lowStockThreshold field from the existing inventory service for store managers.
current_workarounds: Inventory API returns quantity but not the threshold managers compare against.
desired_outcomes: Inventory service output includes lowStockThreshold for every item.
success_proof: A test can assert toInventoryItem returns lowStockThreshold from the source row.
boundaries: Do not change SKU or quantity semantics.
evidence: Existing inventory service maps inventory rows in src/services/inventory.ts.
assumptions: Rows already contain a threshold value from upstream inventory data.
question_model: Where in the workflow does it happen?
How will you know it worked?
What is explicitly out of scope?
What edge cases would make this feel wrong?

scenario_matrix.atomic_task_rule: one scenario surface becomes one session-sized task unless atomicity gate requires more splitting

checks.message: Vision must state the product direction or opportunity in enough detail.
Target user must be explicit before PRD work.
Status quo/current workaround must be captured.
Reflection must challenge premises, assumptions, or alternatives.
Investigation must record evidence or explicit assumptions/TBD-needs-validation.
Question trace or discussion rounds must capture the non-technical answers before PRD.
At least one confirmed requirement is required.
Every requirement should include at least one acceptance scenario.
Demand must be translated into a scenario matrix before executable PRD generation.
Every scenario must include explicit proof that a non-technical user can recognize.
Every scenario must map to at least one implementation surface for atomic task slicing.
Every scenario surface must declare session_budget.max_files <= 2 before executable PRD generation.
Roadmap must define MVP or phased delivery.
Blocking open questions must be resolved or explicitly deferred before PRD.
Explicit user approval with approved=true is required before executable PRD compilation.
Executable PRD requires target files/modules or a bounded execution scope.

next_actions: Demand artifacts are ready for executable PRD compilation.

artifacts.description: Initial product vision, target user, problem, status quo, and opportunity hypothesis.
Premise challenge, assumptions, alternatives, and why this should continue.
Evidence, codebase scout, existing behavior, risks, and validation gaps.
Questioning rounds, answers, decisions, unresolved questions, and deferred ideas.
Demand quality gate, depth verification, and readiness level.
Confirmed requirements, acceptance scenarios, constraints, and out-of-scope boundaries.
Domain language, project context, current state, constraints, and durable decisions.
MVP, sequencing, later phases, dependencies, and risk-driven ordering.
Explicit human approval for PRD compilation and execution readiness.
Executable PRD compiled from approved demand artifacts.
